old_value: {"id":"project-LICHEN-iqxx","title":"[cross-impl] IID derivation inconsistency Python/C vs Rust","description":"Python (identity.py) and C (lichen_link_rx.c using SHA256(pubkey)[:8] + U/L clear) produce different link-local IID from same pubkey than Rust (identity.rs using custom double FNV-1a hash_32 with distinguisher bytes). Violates cross-impl consistency requirement and test vectors for addressing (spec/06-addressing.md, identity test vectors). Same input (pubkey) yields different behavior across impls. Harmonize to canonical SHA256 approach per RFC4291 and update all tests/vectors. Discovered during crossimpl review of recent bd/worker merges.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"agent@lichen-project.dev","created_at":"2026-07-23T02:11:18.084208591Z","created_by":"AI Agent","updated_at":"2026-07-23T02:11:18.084208591Z"}

{"id":"project-LICHEN-iqxx","title":"[cross-impl] IID derivation inconsistency Python/C vs Rust","description":"Python (identity.py) and C (lichen_link_rx.c using SHA256(pubkey)[:8] + U/L clear) produce different link-local IID from same pubkey than Rust (identity.rs using custom double FNV-1a hash_32 with distinguisher bytes). Violates cross-impl consistency requirement and test vectors for addressing (spec/06-addressing.md, identity test vectors). Same input (pubkey) yields different behavior across impls. Harmonize to canonical SHA256 approach per RFC4291 and update all tests/vectors. Discovered during crossimpl review of recent bd/worker merges.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"AI Agent","owner":"agent@lichen-project.dev","created_at":"2026-07-23T02:11:18.084208591Z","created_by":"AI Agent","updated_at":"2026-07-23T02:11:49.853189802Z","started_at":"2026-07-23T02:11:49.853189802Z","lease_expires_at":"2026-07-23T02:16:49.853189802Z","heartbeat_at":"2026-07-23T02:11:49.853189802Z"}
